rules: [P1] Verify status completeness by cross-referencing individual objects against full active sets.
[P1] Cross-reference active order lists with nursing documentation and device-specific logs when confirming the presence or absence of a medical device.
[P1] Explicitly calculate and document time deltas between events and current date in the scratchpad.
[P1] Cite specific dates and codes when justifying actions to ensure traceability.
[P1] Verify medication class, therapeutic intent, status, and expiration dates before flagging orders or initiating new ones.
[P1] Adhere strictly to defined target states and avoid unauthorized clinical substitutions.
[P1] Default to the safest intervention when history is missing or thresholds cannot be calculated.
[P1] Validate the absence of a record by explicitly documenting search scope, parameters, date ranges, and resource types in the scratchpad.
[P1] Define thresholds and logic in the scratchpad to distinguish between informational findings and mandatory action triggers.
[P1] Determine 'most recent' status by comparing all returned entries, not just the first result.
[P1] Append precise temporal parameters to FHIR queries to prioritize server-side filtering.
[P1] Exhaustively audit tool documentation for modification/discontinuation endpoints before declaring an action impossible.
[P1] Execute state-changing operations via tool calls only; never substitute intent statements for programmatic execution.
[P1] Summarize longitudinal data chronologically; ensure assessment, decision, and execution occur in the same turn.
[P1] Map retrieved values against reference ranges and validate temporal sequences.
[P1] Abort intervention if specific temporal constraints or duration conditions cannot be mathematically verified from primary source records.
[P1] Cross-reference active orders against mandatory safety-net protocols and requirements before concluding verification tasks.
[P1] Decouple data retrieval from decision-making; evaluate conditional gates in the scratchpad.
[P1] Execute multi-layered searches across broad resource types to prevent false negatives.
[P1] Execute state-changing tool calls immediately upon confirming intervention criteria.
[P1] Explicitly distinguish between diagnostic procedures and clinical administration records in the scratchpad to prevent data misinterpretation.
[P1] Explicitly document the gap between identified requirements and existing records before executing corrective actions.
[P1] Identify mandatory data gaps and reject outdated or incomplete datasets as insufficient for triggering clinical interventions.
[P1] Verify clinical states via primary source resources rather than summary notes.
[P1] Prioritize reconciliation over new order creation; verify therapeutic equivalents and active orders first.
